name: Adenosine
summary: Adenosine receptor agonist used clinically by IV route for cardiac conduction effects; not a general oral supplement intervention.
description: Adenosine is a clinically used receptor agonist for IV cardiac conduction effects; it is not comparable to general oral supplement interventions.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: Injectable adenosine used for supraventricular tachycardia is contraindicated in second- or third-degree AV block or sick sinus syndrome without a pacemaker and in bronchoconstrictive lung disease such as asthma due to bronchospasm risk (FDA Adenocard prescribing information)
this describes clinical IV drug use rather than oral dietary supplementation since oral safety data do not exist.
